channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran Reports Execution of Revolutionary Guard General Amid Allegations of Espionage
item.description: Iran has reportedly executed General Ismail Qaani, commander of the Quds Force within the Revolutionary Guard, over alleged long-term collaboration with Israel's Mossad. Qaani, aged 67, is said to hav...

item.title: The Hindu Group organises Kolam Competition for Women on International Women’s Day
item.description: To mark International Women’s Day, The Hindu Group is organising a special event titled ‘The Hindu Kolam Kondattam’ at the A.Y.A. Sports and Family Club in Thanjavur on the 8th of March. The competiti...

item.title: Profile of Fr. Gil Dulay: Serving as Chaplain and Lawyer
item.description: Fr. Gil Dulay is a Filipino priest who also serves as a lawyer, blending his vocation in religion with his expertise in law. Known for his dedication to both his parishioners and his legal clients, Fr...

item.title: Military Forces Increasingly Exploit Civilian Security Cameras for Reconnaissance
item.description: Recent research by the security firm Check Point reveals that civilian security cameras are increasingly being targeted as tools for military reconnaissance in conflicts from Ukraine to Iran. Iranian ...

item.title: Government leadership criticised for ignoring working-class hardships
item.description: In recent statements, Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana suggested that critics of his economic policies “have nothing to do” and do not understand the economy, remarks that have sparked widespread con...

item.title: Costco may lower prices if U.S. tariff refunds are granted
item.description: Costco Wholesale announced that it plans to reduce prices on select products if it receives refunds from tariffs imposed under former President Donald Trump's emergency measures, which were recently s...

item.title: SHIMMUTA officially delivers Workers’ Sports Policy to NSC to boost workplace sports
item.description: Dar es Salaam – SHIMMUTA, the federation for sports in Tanzania’s public institutions, agencies and private companies, has officially handed over its Workers’ Sports Policy, called “Sports for the Wel...

item.title: Andrew Hastie calls for review of immigration policy at Perth conservative conference
item.description: Federal Liberal MP Andrew Hastie has renewed his criticism of the Federal Government’s immigration policies, claiming that many Australians are frustrated by current migration levels. Speaking at a Co...

item.title: Decline in Volunteering and Charitable Giving in Canada Over the Last Decade
item.description: Over the past decade, Canada has witnessed a notable decline in volunteerism and charitable giving, raising concerns about the social fabric of the country. Statistics Canada reports that volunteer ho...

item.title: Lionel Messi and Inter Miami Visit White House After MLS Cup Win
item.description: Lionel Messi and his team, Inter Miami, were invited to the White House and recognized by former U.S. President Donald Trump for their triumph in the MLS Cup. The event marked a ceremonial celebration...

item.title: Authorities Close Roads in Pakistan Ahead of Planned Protests Over Iran Situation
item.description: On Friday, major cities in Pakistan, including Karachi and Islamabad, saw significant road closures due to planned nationwide protests by religious parties following recent US-Israel attacks on Iran a...

item.title: Netizens Share Humorous Memes About Kim Jong Un and North Korea Amid Iran-Israel Conflict
item.description: Online communities have been busy creating funny memes about North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong Un, poking fun at his supposed eagerness to join the ongoing Iran-Israel war. Despite North Korea not being ...

item.title: BJP and Congress instruct MPs ahead of Lok Sabha proceedings on Speaker Om Birla
item.description: The second phase of the Budget Session in India’s Parliament is set to begin from March 9 to April 2. Both the ruling Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) and the opposition Congress have issued a three-line ...

item.title: India's energy supply remains stable despite US-Iran tensions, says Hardeep Singh Puri
item.description: Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Hardeep Singh Puri, has reassured citizens that there is no shortage of energy in India despite ongoing geopolitical tensions in West Asia. Speaking to th...

item.title: Trump Supporters in Pennsylvania Show Mixed Reactions to Military Action Against Iran
item.description: Residents in Gettysburg, Pennsylvania, a Republican stronghold, have shown divided opinions following U.S. military strikes against Iran under President Donald Trump. Some voters express concern that ...

item.title: Christina Applegate recalls leaving Brad Pitt for Sebastian Bach at the 1989 MTV Awards
item.description: In her memoir 'You With the Sad Eyes,' Christina Applegate recounts a pivotal moment from the 1989 MTV Video Music Awards. At 17, during the height of her fame from 'Married… with Children,' she atten...

item.title: Remembering Mosiuoa “Terror” Lekota: Champion of Constitutionalism and African Statebuilding
item.description: Mosiuoa “Terror” Lekota, co-founder of COPE, has passed away at the age of 77. He was a committed advocate for South Africa’s democratic transition and a steadfast partner in building the nation’s ins...

item.title: Explosion Injures 18 on Bannu–Miranshah Road in North Waziristan
item.description: At least 18 people sustained injuries in an explosion near the Chashma Sarbandki checkpost on the Bannu–Miranshah main road in North Waziristan, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa on Friday, police and hospital sourc...

item.title: Sen. Jinggoy Estrada faces backlash for inappropriate 'Filipino boyfriend' joke during Gilas prospect's naturalization hearing
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada is under fire from netizens and advocates after making a controversial remark during a Senate hearing on the naturalization of Filipina-American basketball player Elizabeth Mea...

item.title: Scientists Identify a New Genus of Gliding Possum in Papua
item.description: Scientists have formally described a new genus of mammal, a gliding possum named Tous, from the forests of Papua, Indonesia. Previously believed to have gone extinct around 6,000 years ago, this speci...
